content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 26, 1992


Bonnie Streeter
President
Dana Point Action Coalition
P.O. Box 2886
Capistrano Beach, CA  92624

                                          Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                               Our File No. G-92-097

Dear Ms. Streeter:

     This is in response to your letter requesting assistance with
respect to the conduct of officials of the City of Dana Point.  As
we discussed in our telephone conversation of February 25, 1992,
your questions concern areas of law that are not within the
jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission.  The
Commission's jurisdiction is limited to the Political Reform Act
(the "Act"), Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

     In addition, while the Act requires the Commission to provide
formal written advice to any person whose duties are under the Act
in question or to that person's authorized representative,
(Section 83114(b)) the Commission will decline to provide advice
to third parties concerning another person's duties or advice
or advice pertaining to past conduct.  (Regulation 18329(b)(3).)

     However, pursuant to our telephone conversation of
February 25th, we have enclosed general materials concerning the
Act for your information.  In addition, we are returning the
original of the newsletter that you submitted with your letter as
you have requested.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. G-92-097
Page 2

If you have any further questions regarding this matter or
the Political Reform Act, please feel free to contact me at (916)
322-5901. If you wish to file a complaint, please contact the
Commission's Enforcement Division at (916) 322-6441.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa

Enclosures

Jan 20 SWORN 92                                January 21, 1992

                                                        Bonnie Streeter
                                                        President
                                                        Dana Point Action Coalition
                                                        P.O. Box 2886
                                                        Capistrano Beach, CA 92624


March Pong Eu
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear March Pong Eu:

In the summer of 1991 a group of dedicated citizens of Dana
Point, the Dana Point Action Coalition, acquired the legally
prescribed signatures to seek a referendum against the initial
General Plan adopted by the City Council (5-0 vote) for the
newly incorporated city of Dana Point.

The city refused to process our referendum so we had Attorney
Michelle Reinglass file a writ of mandate for us which comes
up in court January 23, 1992 in Department 24 of the Orange
County Superior Court at 9:30 a.m.

That is just a little background. The reason for this letter
is to alert you to the fact that Dana Point city government,
using tax payer money, published Volume 2, Number 4 of the
Dana Point Citynews dated September, 1991 (enclosure #1), and
in this publication, the City countered what they believed to
be the reasons for our referendum.

It is against the fair political practices laws to use tax
payer money to fight a citizens' referendum. Referendum is
one of three POWERS reserved by the people in the State of
California's Constitution.

In addition, the City stated in this same publication that the
General Plan was in effect as of August 14th (1991). It is my
understanding, and correct me if I am wrong, that an initial
general plan cannot be implemented without a zoning ordinance
and an initial zoning ordinance cannot be implemented without
a general plan.

The City published a Draft Zoning Ordinance (DZO) without a
zoning map in the early part of 1991. The City published a
Draft General Plan (DGP) and started holding public hearings.

Page 2
January 21, 1992
March Fong Eu

The citizens complained about the City not supplying a zoning
map and not holding public hearings on both documents because
they felt both documents must be presented in concert to
properly understand them. The City's reply was they had no
legal obligation to hold public hearings on both documents at
the same time and if we, the citizens, wanted a map for the
DZO to use the map in the DGP. Of course the latter is
impossible.

Also in this time frame, the City was implementing a redevel-
opment plan and appointed a redevelopment agency consisting
of the City Council themselves. Once again, I believe it is
a requirement to have a general plan and zoning ordinance in
place before you could implement redevelopment.

Further, the Project Area Committee, one of the largest commit-
tees ever appointed (145), roundly defeated the City's proposed
redevelopment based on inaccurate and false representation of
"blight."

Recently, in October 1991, the City published another DZO this
time including a zoning map. They started public hearings on
same in November still claiming the General Plan to be in effect.

I will appreciate your prompt attention to this letter, and
hope to receive your evaluation or suggestions regarding the
above.

Also enclosed is a citizen produced newsletter, The Dana Point
Watchdog News (enclosure #2).

Sincerely,

Bonnie Streeter

Encl: 1. Dana Point Citynews dtd Sept., 1991
      2. The Dana Point Watchdog News dtd Oct., 1991

cc:  Orange County District Attorney
     CA Coastal Commission
     Cayeah, the wife of Jerry Patterson, the Dana Point
     City Attorney, was elected to the Coastal Commission
     last year. Her last name is Moulton-Patterson.
     Because of a possible conflict of interest she should
     abstain from voting on anything that has to do with
     Dana Point.
